runtime-reporter

Small TypeScript library for centralized runtime messages and type-safe logging/error reporting.

Primary source files:
- src/index.ts: full runtime implementation and exported types
- src/index.test.ts: runtime and type-level tests
- README.md: package docs and examples
- package.json: build/test/lint scripts and published entrypoints

Package entrypoint:
- Exports from src/index.ts
- Published as ESM and CommonJS with generated declaration files

Main API:
- createReporter(messages, options?)
- Reporter methods: message, warn, error, log, fail

Message model:
- Each message type has:
  - code: string literal
  - template: string literal
  - optional tokens: union of token-name string literals
- RuntimeReporterMessages<T> converts the message-type union into a code-to-template object

Behavior:
- message(code, ...args): resolves placeholders and returns formatted string
- warn/error/log(code, ...args): resolve placeholders, call onReport if present, and log only if code exists in messages
- fail(code, ...args): resolves placeholders, calls onReport if present, and throws Error(formattedMessage)
- Missing codes use defaultTemplate, defaulting to "An error occurred"
- Default formatting is "<message> (<code>)"

Token replacement:
- Placeholder syntax is {{ token }}
- Token names are regex-escaped before replacement
- Error token values use error.message
- null and undefined become empty strings

Typing details:
- ReporterTokensArgs decides whether the second argument exists for a given code
- Tokenized messages require the second argument
- Tokenless messages omit the second argument
- tokens: never is treated the same as omitting tokens

Current verified behaviors from tests:
- Single-token and multi-token substitution
- Tokenless messages
- Custom formatMessage
- onReport is not called by message()
- onReport is called by log/warn/error/fail with unformatted message text
- error/warn/log only log when the code exists in the provided messages
- fail always throws

Project commands:
- npm run build
- npm test
- npm run lint
- npm run format

Do not infer features beyond the code and tests in this repository.
